routes.name: Fisher Chimneys
watch_out: Loose rock on summit pyramid (3/4-class summit block); expect rockfall from above when congested
Late-season glacier hardness on Upper Curtis Glacier; hard ice requires crampons; aluminum adequate but conditions tenuous
Serac/avalanche debris fields in Shuksan Arm approach; spring/early summer activity; rain can trigger additional slides
Route-finding complexity through tortuous chimney system; wrong gully has moss/lichen-covered rock; easy to lose route in early season snow
Crevasse/bergschrund exposure on White Salmon Glacier; temporary bridges deteriorate late season; open crevasses by August

routes.name: North Face (Crystal Glacier Route)
watch_out: Active Hanging Glacier avalanches (very large volumes); bus-sized to house-sized slides sweep cirque; debris reaches base of climbing area; most active June-August
Serac calving over snow chutes (Crystal Glacier); bus-sized ice blocks above climbers; ice falls into crevasses below
Steep snow slope avalanche exposure (40-50 degrees); sloughing hazard; transitions to steeper sections near shoulder; worst post-rain or high-sun
Route exposure and altitude/weather patterns; high alpine exposure at 9000+ ft; rapid weather changes; strong winds possible
Crevasse fields (Crystal/Hanging Glacier interface); well-bridged in early season; open and extensive by late season; progressive through season

routes.name: Price Glacier
watch_out: Massive overhanging seracs with ice calving; bus-sized piece documented calving above climber; TV-sized rock/ice in gullies; seracs impend over 8500-8800 ft
Extensive crevasse/bergschrund field; early season: solid snowpack bridges; late season: extensive bare ice, forced climbing in/out of overhanging sections
Late-season ice climbing difficulty and exposure (August-September); 50+ degree ice possible; requires ice tools and screws; mixed alpine terrain; AI2-3 grade
Rockfall from Nooksack Tower and surrounding peaks; TV-sized rocks documented; loose rock in couloirs; afternoon sun triggers more activity
Turnaround strategy and descent complexity; most competent alpinists need 2-3 days; descent typically via Sulphide or White Salmon Glacier, not ascent route

summary.coverage: Mount Shuksan primary alternatives beyond standard Sulphide Glacier approach
summary.data_quality: Multi-source verified (Mountain Project, SummitPost, Beckey Alpine Guide, 2024-2026 trip reports)
